mission: Publish one fail-closed runtime topology publication graph so bundles, parity, gateway surfaces, frontend manifests, and design/runtime bindings can drift only as explicit blocked findings.

gap_resolutions.summary: par_099 generates one publication matrix that resolves topology, gateway, frontend, surface-binding, and design-bundle tuples from the current repo state instead of leaving those relations implied across separate earlier tasks.
Design publication bundles did not previously carry a topology-publication seam, so par_099 adds a machine-checkable derived binding row without changing seq_052 bundle identity.

follow_on_dependencies.title: Later governance surfaces may present richer parity and publication detail
follow_on_dependencies.bounded_seam: par_099 publishes the machine-readable topology-publication graph and drift findings now. Later UI tasks may enrich governance presentation without redefining drift law.

driftCategoryDefinitions.label: Missing manifest binding
driftCategoryDefinitions.description: A bundle, gateway, frontend manifest, binding, publication, or design row points to an object that is absent.

driftCategoryDefinitions.label: Gateway to undeclared workload
driftCategoryDefinitions.description: A route or gateway points to a workload family that is not declared by the approved runtime topology tuple.

driftCategoryDefinitions.label: Undeclared trust-boundary crossing
driftCategoryDefinitions.description: A gateway or browser publication points across a trust boundary that is not present in the approved topology tuple.

driftCategoryDefinitions.label: Tenant isolation mismatch
driftCategoryDefinitions.description: Gateway, route-publication, and bundle rows disagree on tenant-isolation posture for the same surface tuple.

driftCategoryDefinitions.label: Stale audience-surface runtime binding
driftCategoryDefinitions.description: Frontend manifests, audience bindings, or surface publications still point at stale or planned runtime-publication bundle refs.

driftCategoryDefinitions.label: Design bundle wrong topology tuple
driftCategoryDefinitions.description: A design publication bundle is bound to the wrong runtime-publication bundle or topology tuple.

driftCategoryDefinitions.label: Route publication withdrawn
driftCategoryDefinitions.description: A route publication is blocked or withdrawn while the publication graph still tries to present it as part of the active tuple.

publicationScenarios.title: Missing manifest binding
publicationScenarios.description: A frontend manifest points to a runtime binding row that does not exist in the publication graph.

driftFindings.message: Frontend manifest points at a missing runtime binding.

publicationScenarios.title: Gateway to undeclared workload
publicationScenarios.description: A route publication widens into a workload family that is not declared by the approved topology tuple.

driftFindings.message: Route publication reaches an undeclared workload family.

publicationScenarios.title: Undeclared trust-boundary crossing
publicationScenarios.description: A gateway surface reaches across a trust boundary that does not exist in the approved topology tuple.

driftFindings.message: Gateway surface crosses an undeclared trust boundary.
Gateway surface references a trust-boundary row that is absent from the topology manifest.

publicationScenarios.title: Tenant isolation mismatch
publicationScenarios.description: A route publication no longer matches the tenant-isolation mode declared by its primary gateway surface.

driftFindings.message: Route publication tenant-isolation mode drifted from the gateway surface.

publicationScenarios.title: Stale audience-surface runtime binding
publicationScenarios.description: Browser manifests and surface publications still point at planned publication refs instead of the active runtime tuple.

driftFindings.message: Frontend manifest points at a stale runtime-publication bundle ref.
Audience surface runtime binding is not exact for the active runtime tuple.
Audience surface publication is stale or tied to the wrong runtime-publication bundle.

publicationScenarios.title: Design bundle wrong topology tuple
publicationScenarios.description: A design publication bundle is rebound to the wrong runtime-publication bundle and topology hash.

driftFindings.message: Design publication bundle is bound to the wrong runtime-publication or topology tuple.

publicationScenarios.title: Withdrawn route publication
publicationScenarios.description: One route publication is withdrawn while the bundle still tries to keep the surface in the active publication graph.

driftFindings.message: Route publication is withdrawn or blocked for the active topology tuple.
